# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

redis_replication:
    master: &id1
        service: redisMasterServiceName # name of your existing Redis Master Service, e.g., "myRedisMasterservice" 
        port: <port>                    # default is '6379' for standard setups but can be different if needed  
    
    slave1: &id2                         
        service: redisSlaveServiceName# name of your existing Redis Slaves Service, e.g., "myRedisSlaverservice" 
        port: <port>                    # default is '6378' for standard setups but can be different if needed  
    
    slave2: &id3                         
         service: redisSlaveServiceName# name of your existing Redis Slaves Service, e.g., "myRedisSlaverservice" 
        port: <port>                    # default is '6378' for standard setups but can be different if needed  
    
    replicas_per_slave : 1                # Number of slave instances you want to create, e.g., "2", by setting it higher than one the number will increase replication factor (write performance) and consume more resources in your setup due default configuration is set at '0' for this option  
    
    min-slaves: 1                        # Minimum Number of Slave Instances, e.g., "2", to ensure write operations succeed even if some slaves are down or non existent (e.g.: Redis slave server not running) set it at '0' for this option  
    
    masterauth : <master_password>       # Password required by redis-trib, e.g., "myPassword"  when setting up the Master and Slaves in a cluster mode (default is empty string), you must provide password to access Redis Cluster Mode without authentication
